@graph.name: BMW models fitted with the BMW S55

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW S55 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned S55 typically costs £7,000–£12,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], with final price varying by specification (standard, Competition, CS) and any included upgrades. Fitting adds £780–£1,300 depending on workshop rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW S55 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S55 earns 3.5★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED]. It is significantly more robust than its predecessor (S65 V8) but has known weak points: the crank‑hub can spin and rod‑bearings can wear, especially on tuned cars.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common BMW S55 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Crank‑hub spin is the most catastrophic — the timing chain hub can slip, causing valve‑to‑piston contact. Typically occurs on high‑torque, high‑revving or tuned cars from 40,000–80,000 miles. A preventative hub fix costs £1,500–£2,500.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the S55 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S55 powers the F80 M3 saloon, F82/F83 M4 coupe/convertible, and the F87 M2 Competition. It was also used in the M4 GTS, CS and M2 CS variants.

mainEntity.name: How long does the BMW S55 engine last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With meticulous maintenance — including 5,000‑mile oil changes and a crank‑hub fix before tuning — an S55 can exceed 150,000 reliable miles. Rod‑bearing wear is less common than on the S65 but should be inspected at 60,000 miles.

hero.h1: BMW S55 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW S55 is the high‑performance twin‑turbo straight‑six from the F80 M3 and F82 M4 — a potent track‑bred engine, but crank hub and rod bearing issues are well‑documented. Backed by strong UK enthusiast demand [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: 3 models (M3 F80, M4 F82/F83, M2 Competition F87)

verdictRating.confidence: 75% confidence — based on extensive owner‑forum data, specialist rebuild reports, and parts‑supply validation [ESTIMATED].

rows.score: 14 (3.5★ × 4 = 14 — a robust turbo engine, but crank‑hub and rod‑bearing issues are real)
12 (Mix of straightforward fixes (oil leaks, sensors) and involved ones (crank‑hub, bearings))
16 (M‑specific parts are widely available but expensive; strong aftermarket support)
8 (Recon cost (~£9,500) vs conservative £15,000 lowest host value → 63% (60‑74% band))

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The S55 is a potent, track‑ready twin‑turbo six — but crank‑hub and rod‑bearing risks are real — budget for preventative work or a full inspection.
verdictRating.bestFor: Track‑day enthusiasts and those seeking a high‑revving M car with strong tuning potential.
verdictRating.avoidIf: No documented crank‑hub fix or rod‑bearing inspection above 50,000 miles.

rows.variantBadge: M4, M4 Competition, M4 CS, M4 GTS

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Exclusive to BMW — no cross‑brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an S55 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
